objective: By the end of the lesson, learners will have practiced and developed the sub-skills of listening for specific information about online identity protection and speaking to present a structured personal response.

stages.stage_aim: To spark curiosity about online identity theft through a real-world news clip
stages.procedure:
Part 1 — Play the YouTube clip (3 min). Students watch without taking notes.
Part 2 — In pairs, students discuss: "What themes did you see in this video? What kind of problems did it show?" (2 min). Elicit 3-4 ideas in feedback.
Part 3 — In new pairs: "Has anything like this ever happened to you or someone you know?" (1 min). Elicit 2-3 responses.
Transition: "Today we're going to look at how we can protect our own identity online."

stages.stage_aim: To pre-teach key vocabulary for the listening text
stages.procedure:
Present five words: phishing, unsecured, decode, symbols, curiosity.
For each word: write on board, model pronunciation, drill chorally and individually.
Concept-check each word with simple questions.
Leave words on the board for students to refer to during the listening.

stages.stage_aim: To help learners read for specific information and check their answers through listening
stages.procedure:
Part 1 — Students open OFD2 p10. Read the "Identity Alert" webpage silently and complete Exercise 1 (choose the correct number for each gap). (5 min)
Part 2 — In pairs, students read the Exercise 3 questions (p11) and try to predict the answers based on the webpage. (2 min)
Part 3 — Play audio. Students listen, check their Exercise 1 answers, and answer the Exercise 3 questions. (8 min)
Part 4 — Whole-class feedback. Go through Exercise 1 answers, then Exercise 3. Clarify any confusion. (3 min)

stages.stage_aim: To practise asking and answering personal questions about online safety
stages.procedure:
Part 1 — Teacher models the activity with a strong student. Ask: "Do you share your passwords with your friends?" Elicit answer and follow-up. Show vocabulary words (privacy, hacker, hotspot, PIN, install) on the board. (2 min)
Part 2 — In pairs, students take turns asking each other the Exercise 4 questions using the highlighted words from the webpage. Teacher monitors and takes notes. (4 min)
Part 3 — Ask 2-3 pairs to share one interesting thing they learned about their partner. (1 min)

stages.stage_aim: To diagnose learners' ability to produce a structured short talk about identity theft
stages.procedure:
Part 1 — Show the Structure Your Talk slide. Explain the three parts: thesis (main idea), reasons (1-2 reasons why), example (a real example). Introduce transition words: "First...", "Also...", "For example...". (2 min)
Part 2 — Students prepare a 1-minute talk on: "What is identity theft? Why is it a problem? Give an example you know." (2 min)
Part 3 — 2-3 volunteers deliver their talks to the class. Teacher listens and takes notes (diagnostic — do not correct or grade). (2 min)

stages.stage_aim: To consolidate learning and reflect on key takeaways
stages.procedure:
Part 1 — In pairs, students share one new word they learned today and one thing they can do to protect their identity online. (1 min)
Part 2 — Teacher gives brief positive feedback on the diagnostic talks (strengths only). (1 min)
Part 3 — Preview: "Next lesson, we'll look at how to make our talks more powerful using emphasis strategies." (1 min)
